articles.title: In Nalanda, faith turned fatal
articles.description: On March 31, over 10,000 devotees, mostly women, thronged the Sheetla Mata temple in Nalanda, Bihar. The temple, which normally holds 500-1,000 people, was overwhelmed, triggering a stampede that claimed nine lives. Victims’ families allege that priests let devotees enter through the exit gate after collecting money. Amit Bhelari reports on how poor crowd management and inadequate arrangements worsened the tragedy

articles.description: “Standing in Kashmir, I want to convey to our neighbouring country that POK, an integral part of India, will witness ‘ghar wapsi’ and rejoin India. People of POK want to have a referendum there after witnessing the current development pace in Kashmir,” says Mr. Ilyasi

topStories.title: NSUI workers clash with police over RSS-linked event in Rajasthan University
topStories.description: The student wing of the Opposition Congress took exception to a programme being organised by Marudhara Nari Sashaktikaran Sangathan at the humanities auditorium on the university campus, saying it was meant to promote the RSS ideology on the pretext of deliberating on women’s empowerment

topStories.title: "We're In War": Trump Says Downing Of US Aircraft Won't Affect Iran Talks
topStories.description: US President Donald Trump has asserted that the destruction of an American military aircraft will have no bearing on diplomatic discussions with Iran, according to a report by NBC News.

topStories.title: Pakistan jibes become central to BJP’s Assam poll narrative
topStories.description: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has been attacking Congress leader Gaurav Gogoi over his alleged Pakistan connections, his latest barb was aimed at the mother of a candidate whom he accused of sympathising with ‘anti-national’ elements

topStories.title: Cuba releases over 2,000 prisoners amid mounting US pressure
topStories.description: There have been emotional scenes in Cuba where 2,000 prisoners are being pardoned and released from jail.

topStories.title: Tesla’s Texas factory workforce reportedly shrunk 22% in 2025
topStories.description: Tesla's headcount fell from 21,191 workers to 16,506 workers in 2025, according to a report, as it grappled with its second straight year of declining sales.

topStories.title: Meet the elite US teams tasked with combat rescues
topStories.description: Air Force Pararescue units who specialise in CSAR missions are among the most highly trained in the US military.

topStories.title: Survivors of Iran bridge attack recall terror of US-Israeli strike
topStories.description: Survivors of an Israeli-US attack on Iran’s biggest road bridge have spoken of their terror as missiles hit it.

topStories.title: Amit Shah promises UCC in Assam with exemptions for tribal communities
topStories.description: Amit Shah promotes BJP's return in Assam, pledging a Uniform Civil Code while excluding tribals, and accuses Congress of neglecting tribal welfare.

topStories.description: The Supreme Court resolved a decade-long matrimonial dispute, granting divorce and settling all claims between the couple while quashing 61 related cases.

dailyNews.title: Iran rejects US' 48-hour ceasefire proposal, push was made by another country - Report
dailyNews.description: Iran rejects US' 48-hour ceasefire proposal, push was made by another country - Report

dailyNews.title: For some around Trump, war on Iran is a Christian calling
dailyNews.description: Religion is playing a role in the US conflict with Iran. President Donald Trump has met with Christian pastors who offered blessings. Some officials view the conflict as a divine mission. This echoes historical religious wars. Iran's government is also religiously rooted. The US is invoking religious themes in its foreign policy.

dailyNews.title: Iran Refuses To Meet US Officials In Pak, Ceasefire Efforts Hit Wall: Report
dailyNews.description: The push, led by Pakistan, has failed to yield a breakthrough, with Tehran formally notifying mediators that it is unwilling to send officials to Islamabad for talks in the coming days.

dailyNews.title: Two-worded message & an old pic - Vanessa Trump breaks her silence on boyfriend Tiger Woods' crash, DUI arrest
dailyNews.description: Tiger Woods made his and Vanessa Trump's relationship public on Instagram in March 2025

dailyNews.title: US unemployment rate drops despite economic uncertainty and Iran war
dailyNews.description: White House says March jobs report 'blew out expectations' with gains made in the healthcare and construction sectors.

dailyNews.title: One crew member rescued by special forces says US officials after Iran shoots down American F-15 fighter jet
dailyNews.description: Most Americans favor a swift end to US involvement in the conflict, according to a recent poll. The downing of an F-15E fighter jet in Iran led to one crew member's rescue, with ongoing concerns about potential US military personnel capture affecting public opinion.

dailyNews.title: NATO Chief To Visit US Next Week Amid Trump's Threat To Quit Alliance
dailyNews.description: Trump has suggested he is considering quitting the 77-year-old military alliance due to the response by European nations to his war.

dailyNews.title: What we know about the US fighter jet shot down in Iran
dailyNews.description: The White House has confirmed Donald Trump has been briefed on the shooting down of a US fighter jet in Iran.

dailyNews.title: 'Capture Enemy Pilots Alive': Iran Channel Issues Reward After US Jet Downed
dailyNews.description: An Iranian channel announced a reward for civilians if the pilots were captured and handed over to the police and military forces.

dailyNews.title: US rescues one crew member of F-15E fighter jet shot down by Iran; Tehran asks for civilian help: Report
dailyNews.description: According to the reports, Israel is helping the US with search and rescue operation of the crew member.

dailyNews.title: WHO Confirms Over 20 Strikes On Iranian Health Facilities As Global Alarm Grows
dailyNews.description: Neither the US nor Israel has publicly commented on the specific strikes against medical facilities.

topStories.title: New York Times goes viral for erroneous print headline on Trump's NATO exit threat
topStories.description: The New York Times went viral for botching the name of NATO in a print headline about President Donald Trump's ongoing threat to the treaty organization.

topStories.description: Stephen A. Smith weighed in on President Trump attending Supreme Court arguments on birthright citizenship, saying he doesn’t blame the move and calling the issue significant.

dailyNews.title: Trump signs executive order on college sports to regain control of NIL
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump signed a second executive order for college sports that is reportedly aimed at expanding NCAA control over athletes and NIL issues.

dailyNews.title: Trump Seeks $152 Million to Begin to Turn Alcatraz Back Into a Prison
dailyNews.description: The plan faces significant local political opposition and the dilapidated state of the site poses considerable logistical challenges.

dailyNews.title: What has changed in Venezuela since Trump ousted Maduro?
dailyNews.description: U.S. military forces captured then-Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro three months ago. His vice president, Delcy Rodríguez, has taken over as the country's acting leader, and President Trump said on Wednesday that the U.S. and Venezuela are working well together. CBS News national correspondent Lilia Luciano has more.

dailyNews.title: Maryland Gov. Wes Moore, who served in Afghanistan War, says Iran conflict is "very similar"
dailyNews.description: Maryland Gov. Wes Moore told CBS News that he believes the U.S. is heading toward "another forever war" in Iran, paid for by Americans. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe, who conducted the interview, joins "The Takeout" to unpack the governor's remarks.

dailyNews.title: Trump's prime-time Iran address, Bondi's departure and more: Recapping the week in politics
dailyNews.description: Jeff Mason, Washington correspondent for Bloomberg, and Tia Mitchell, Washington bureau chief of the Atlanta Journal-Constitution, join "The Takeout" to unpack some of the week's biggest political moments.

dailyNews.title: Hegseth ousts Army chief of staff in latest Pentagon purge
dailyNews.description: Army Chief of Staff Randy George is out of a job as part of the Trump administration's most recent Pentagon purge. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson has the details.

dailyNews.title: Trump signs executive order to expand NCAA's control over college sports
dailyNews.description: The executive order is designed to increase the NCAA's control over college sports, and threatens to remove federal funding for colleges and universities that don't comply with NCAA rules.

dailyNews.title: Trump's national security team assembles at White House amid downed jet search and rescue mission
dailyNews.description: President Trump's national security team convened at the White House on Friday amid the ongoing search for a second crew member aboard a fighter jet downed over Iran. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Eleanor Watson report.

dailyNews.title: Trump threatens civilian infrastructure in Iran after it downed a U.S. fighter jet
dailyNews.description: It has been over a month since the war in Iran began, and President Trump is now threatening attacks on civilian infrastructure. This comes after strikes destroyed an Iranian bridge on Thursday. CBS News Middle East contributor Courtney Kealy reports.

dailyNews.title: Trump signs memo directing DHS to pay all employees during shutdown
dailyNews.description: The president signed a similar memo last week directing pay for TSA workers.

dailyNews.title: Firings in Trump 1.0 vs Trump 2.0
dailyNews.description: NBC News' Garrett Haake joins "Here's the Scoop" to discuss what the nexts steps might be for President Trump after the recent firings of Kristi Noem and Pam Bondi.

dailyNews.title: Trump says downing of U.S. jet won't impact Iran negotiations
dailyNews.description: Trump says downing of U.S. jet won't impact Iran negotiations
